_meta.note: LOCAL 4-official multi-jurisdiction batch (rick-west PULLED for 2-way slug-collision blocker — VA Chesapeake Mayor id=504 collides with OK State Representative id=5483, same slug 'rick-west'; engine's by_slug dict keys the OK record so applying VA-Mayor research would WRONGLY rewrite the OK State Rep; needs separate slug-disambiguation pass mirroring the mike-thompson 3-way pattern, recommend renaming OK rep to rick-west-ok or rick-west-ok-state-rep). (1) Dana Blickley FL Brevard Property Appraiser evidence_local — re-elected unopposed 2024, term to Jan 2029; 1 HARD TRUE christian_liberty.q0 (March 2024 SCDaily quote 'my relationship with Jesus Christ, through which all things are possible'); all other cells NULL with HIGH-BAR discipline (homestead/SOH education = administrative not affirmative anti-deficit advocacy). Engine classifies tier=state (regex doesn't include 'property appraiser'); refuse_state_overreach cells ignored as out-of-tier — christian_liberty.q0 TRUE still applies. (2) Lisa Cullen FL Brevard Tax Collector evidence_local — 84.8% Aug-2024 GOP primary, term to Jan 2029; 1 HARD TRUE christian_liberty.q0 (Hometown News 2024 candidate questionnaire self-statement '34 years on ministry staff of a local church'); all other cells NULL (husband retired BCSO Lt is biographical not public_justice.q0 affirmative LE advocacy; office's CWL issuance = FDACS-statutory not self_defense advocacy). Same tier=state classification as blickley (regex doesn't include 'tax collector'); christian_liberty.q0 TRUE still applies. (3) Mickie Kellum FL Cape Canaveral STALENESS FIX status=former — Nov 2022 term expired Nov 2025; current Cape Canaveral roster on capecanaveral.gov/community_/elected_officials.php shows Kay Jackson as Mayor Pro Tem + Andrea King and Tom Shoriak as new council members in the seats expiring 2025+; Kellum NOT retained; older /your_elected_officials.php page is stale and superseded. 30 NULL through tenure (Jan 2025 3-2 vote firing City Manager Morley is management dispute not pro-LE q0 advocacy; her 'small-town feeling and keeping taxes low' 2022 candidate quote is rhetorical not a roll-call). (4) Kim Davis FL Cape Canaveral City Council roster_verified — D-affiliated per BrevardDems Elected Officials page (party flip from None to D); retired BCSO deputy biographical NOT public_justice.q0 TRUE per HIGH BAR (mirrors Cocoa Hearn-FF/EMT NULL pattern); 30 NULL cells. Currency caveat: 2024 community-FB-group post 'Kim Davis suffers stroke' noted but city site still lists her in June 2026; not status-changing without verification, flagged for next sweep. NOT yet status=former. Cape Canaveral has NOT issued Pride proclamation + NOT passed 2A sanctuary verified per prior Morrison/Willis batch + this batch's searches; FL SB 168 (2019) + HB 543 (2023) preempt at state level. Confidence calls follow refine-scorecard SKILL.md (high bar for TRUE, null where no public position, never infer from party).

profile.religion: Publicly professed Christian — March 2024 Space Coast Daily Women's History Month quote: 'thankful for Grace, the precious gift of life, and my relationship with Jesus Christ, through which all things are possible' (denomination/specific church not identified in public sources)
profile.background: Dana Blickley is the elected constitutional officer Property Appraiser of Brevard County, Florida (Republican). First elected November 2012 defeating incumbent Jim Ford by 14 points in the GOP primary; re-elected unopposed in 2016, 2020, and 2024; current term runs through January 2029. Education: BA and MA in Public Administration, University of Central Florida. Certified Florida Appraiser (CFA). 30+ years in mass appraisal / assessment administration; worked 20 years inside BCPAO (fired in 2006 by then-incumbent Ford). Brevard County resident 30+ years; married with two children; father was a Marine. Civic: board member of National Veterans Homeless Support (NVHS) since 2012; established a scholarship endowment for veterans at Eastern Florida State College. 2012 candidacy backed by the Liberty Catalyst Fund (Republican Liberty Caucus of Central East Florida + Space Coast Patriots + Brevard 9/12 grassroots coalition). Active 2025 speaking circuit: Space Coast Patriots, Merritt Island civic groups, Viera Q&A, Republican Women's Federation.

dana-blickley.notes_set: ARCHETYPE: Administrative constitutional officer (county property appraiser) with one HARD-cited Christian-faith profession but no other documented public positions on Pillar A/B cells. 1 HARD TRUE (christian_liberty.q0) + 0 FALSE = 100% on the single scoreable cell — sparse-data trap noted but evidence_local is honest given the cited single position. The administrative office (county property valuation under FL Statute 195) has no statutory engagement with abortion, marriage, education curricula, election ballots, law enforcement, immigration, or 2A — most rubric cells genuinely don't intersect her duties. HIGH BAR discipline preserved: her recurring homestead/SOH/portability education program (presents to Space Coast Patriots, Brevard 9/12, RUSH, Merritt Island civic groups, Viera Q&A) is administrative/statutory outreach — NOT affirmative anti-deficit / anti-tax-hike advocacy in the rubric's sense — so economic_stewardship.q2 left NULL not TRUE. 2023 budget-withholding dispute (Commissioner Tobia alleged FL Statute 195.087(1)(a) violation for not providing full FY23-24 budget to BoCC) was an inter-agency transparency dispute where she was the LESS-transparent party toward her local elected board — NOT scored as refuse_state_overreach subsidiarity (would be reverse of subsidiarity). Brevard BoCC denied $5,000 Space Coast Pride-Fest grant in 2025 (Commissioner Delaney) — county-level non-Pride posture but Blickley took no documented public position. Christian_liberty.q0 TRUE based on her own published 2024 quote about Jesus Christ relationship — this is hard evidence per the SKILL's 'public profession' definition. Conservative-coalition signal (Liberty Catalyst Fund 2012; ongoing Space Coast Patriots speaking) corroborates Republican identity but is not specific-cell evidence. Currency: re-elected unopposed Aug 2024 + 2025 speaking schedule = high-confidence still in office.

profile.religion: Self-stated 'on ministry staff of a local church for 34 years' (Hometown News Brevard 2024 candidate questionnaire) — denomination and specific church not publicly identified
profile.background: Lisa Cullen, CFC, is the elected constitutional officer Tax Collector of Brevard County, Florida (Republican). Born in Logan, West Virginia; family relocated to Brevard County early 1980s. Education: Merritt Island High School; A.A. Brevard Community College / Eastern Florida State College; B.S. Accounting & Finance, University of Central Florida. Career: began at the Brevard Tax Collector's Office front counter in 1985; Certified Florida Collector (CFC) since February 2009. First elected November 2008; assumed office January 2009; re-elected unopposed in 2012, 2016, and 2020. August 20, 2024 Republican primary: defeated County Commissioner Rita Pritchett 84.8% to 15.9%; write-in opponent Daniel Edwin Lopez withdrew early September 2024 making her re-elected unopposed in the general for a term to January 2029. President of the Florida Tax Collectors Association 2014-2015. Married to high-school sweetheart Robert Cullen, a RETIRED Brevard County Sheriff's Office Lieutenant. EFSC Foundation Board (2012-2018, Board Chair); Michael-Gene Basketball Foundation; National Homeless Veterans Support. 2016 TDC Portrait of Excellence; 2022 EFSC Distinguished Alumni. Rated 'A' by Ultra MAGA Patriots Brevard County (May 2024).

lisa-cullen.notes_set: ARCHETYPE: Administrative constitutional officer (county tax collector) with one HARD self-statement of multi-decade church ministry role and no other public positions on rubric cells. 1 HARD TRUE (christian_liberty.q0 — 34-year ministry staff self-statement) + 0 FALSE = 100% on the single scoreable cell — sparse-data trap noted but evidence_local is honest given the cited self-statement. HIGH BAR discipline preserved: husband Robert is retired BCSO Lieutenant (strong personal LE tie) but that is BIOGRAPHICAL not affirmative pro-LE policy advocacy — public_justice.q0 NULL not TRUE (mirrors Cocoa Hearn FF/EMT biographical NULL pattern from prior batch). Office's mission slogan 'Transparency and Accountability; Every Day in Every Way' is administrative tone, not specific Pillar A/B advocacy. Office issues Concealed Weapon Licenses at 4 branches under FDACS partnership — statutory implementation NOT a 2A advocacy stance (self_defense cells NULL). Office implements FL HB 1451 (2023) driver-license posture and FL HB 543 (2023) constitutional carry by statutory compliance — not policy advocacy. Brevard BoCC pursued 'Bill of Rights Sanctuary County' ordinance late 2024 (Commissioner Feltner, pulled Dec 3 2024 agenda); Cullen took no public position. Brevard BoCC unanimously passed anti-sanctuary-city resolution 2018 (Commissioner Tobia); Cullen took no documented sign-on. COVID-era office took 'extra precautions' with PPE/distancing — standard compliance, not mandate-refusal (refuse_state_overreach.q2 NULL). Ultra MAGA Patriots 'A' rating is THIRD-PARTY grassroots assessment, not specific-cell advocacy. The 34-year ministry staff self-statement is HARD evidence of long-term Christian-church involvement meeting SKILL's public-profession definition — TRUE for christian_liberty.q0 with the caveat that denomination is unverified. Currency: 84.8% Aug-2024 GOP primary + January 2029 term = high-confidence still in office.

profile.background: Maria 'Mickie' Kellum is a FORMER Mayor Pro Tem / City Council member, City of Cape Canaveral, Florida. Elected November 2019; re-elected November 2022 for a term ending November 2025. STALENESS FIX: As of 2026-06-05 the live capecanaveral.gov authoritative Elected Officials page (/community_/elected_officials.php) lists Mayor Wes Morrison, Mayor Pro Tem Kay Jackson, and Council Members Andrea King, Tom Shoriak, Don Willis — Kellum is NOT on the current council. Older stale /your_elected_officials.php page still shows Kellum as Mayor Pro Tem and is superseded. The Nov 4, 2025 election filled the two seats expiring 2025; Kellum was not retained. 30+ years Cape Canaveral resident; widow of retired tugboat captain Jeff Kellum; mother of two, grandmother of four; former small-business owner. Heavy civic engagement: Veterans Park revitalization, monthly VFW Post 10131 breakfast volunteering, former President of VFW Auxiliary, former Cape Canaveral Culture & Leisure Services Advisory Board, Project Manager/Treasurer for Ais Village Trail Public Archaeology Organization, Space Coast Little League board.

mickie-kellum.notes_set: STALENESS FIX: status set from active to former — Kellum's Nov 2022 term expired Nov 2025; current capecanaveral.gov/community_/elected_officials.php authoritative roster shows Kay Jackson as Mayor Pro Tem + Andrea King and Tom Shoriak as new council members in the seats expiring 2025+. Kellum was not retained at the Nov 4, 2025 election. (Note: an older /government/your_elected_officials.php page still shows Kellum as Mayor Pro Tem — that page is stale and authoritative roster supersedes it.) ARCHETYPE: Veterans-Park / VFW-focused civic-engagement small-city Mayor Pro Tem with limited public footprint on Pillar A/B cells. 0 TRUE / 0 FALSE = no scoreable positions through tenure. 30 NULL — most rubric cells lack any documented HIGH-BAR position. Notable on-record items that do NOT qualify as TRUE/FALSE per SKILL discipline: (a) 2022 candidate quote 'I want to make sure we can keep that small-town feeling and keeping taxes low… infrastructure… pipes and sewer system… stormwater runoff out the lagoon' — generic rhetorical not specific roll-call (economic_stewardship.q2 NULL not TRUE); (b) January 2025 voted with Morrison + Jackson 3-2 to fire City Manager Todd Morley over public-records / code-enforcement failures (Davis + Willis dissented) — management dispute NOT affirmative pro-LE advocacy (public_justice.q0 NULL); (c) NOT on FLC Home Rule Heroes list 2022-2025 (refuse_state_overreach.q3 NULL — contrast Don Willis who IS a Home Rule Hero). Cape Canaveral has NOT issued Pride proclamations + NOT passed 2A sanctuary resolution per prior Morrison/Willis batch and this batch's searches; FL SB 168 (2019) preempts sanctuary cities + FL HB 543 (2023) constitutional carry at state level. Church bio 'Our Saviors Parish' is too unspecific for HARD christian_liberty.q0 TRUE — denomination and congregation unverified, so NULL. Status=former is the win this batch — record now exits active queue under runbook's active() filter.

profile.background: Kim Davis is City Council member, City of Cape Canaveral, Florida (Democrat). NOT to be confused with Kim Davis the Rowan County KY clerk who refused same-sex marriage licenses in 2015. Florida native; one of the first babies born at Cape Canaveral Hospital; retired Brevard County Sheriff's Office deputy (served Cape Canaveral in early 1980s); former small-business owner; mother of a child with a disability. Party affiliation Democrat per Brevard County Democratic Executive Committee Elected Officials page. First elected and reelected in 2022 against Mickie Kellum + Gregory McDonald + Carroll Sanders for two seats; won. 2022 term implies term-end ~November 2025; she remains listed on city site in 2026. Civic record: Cape Canaveral Advisory Board for Culture and Leisure Services; Project Manager/Treasurer Ais Village Trail Public Archaeology Organization; former President VFW Auxiliary; Junior Vice Auxiliary Post 348 American Legion; Trustee VFW Auxiliary; Board member Space Coast Little League. Currency caveat: 2024 Cape Canaveral community FB-group post 'Councilmember Kim Davis suffers stroke' noted but city page still lists her in June 2026; no public evidence of resignation found — flagged for next sweep verification.

January 2025 Davis voted AGAINST firing City Manager Todd Morley with Don Willis as the two dissenters in the 3-2 vote — a management dispute, not rubric-cell evidence. NOT on FLC Home Rule Heroes list (refuse_state_overreach.q3 NULL — contrast Don Willis). Cape Canaveral has NOT issued Pride proclamations + NOT passed 2A sanctuary resolution per prior Morrison/Willis batch + this batch's searches; FL SB 168 (2019) + FL HB 543 (2023) preempt sanctuary + gun-control at state level. No church/faith affiliation publicly disclosed (christian_liberty.q0 NULL — the Apostolic Pentecostal hits are the KY clerk, not this Davis).
